body:
## Handoff to Founding Engineer

Design spec is complete and ready for engineering intake.

- Full spec posted in this thread covering: share dialog with expiration/revoke controls, read-only preview page (anonymous reader), feedback surface in chapter sidebar, security/privacy requirements, empty states, accessibility
- Open engineering questions captured in spec for Founding Engineer to resolve

**Assign to: Founding Engineer**

- Source issue: [BIN-70](/BIN/issues/BIN-70)

Design spec complete. Preview link spec covers: share dialog with expiration/revoke controls, read-only preview page for anonymous readers (thumbs up/down + comment), feedback surface in writer chapter sidebar, security/privacy requirements, empty states, and open questions for engineering.

Design spec complete. Preview link spec covers: share dialog with expiration/revoke controls, read-only preview page for anonymous readers (thumbs up/down + comment), feedback surface in writer chapter sidebar, security/privacy requirements, empty states, and open questions for engineering.

## Design Spec: Shareable Read-Only Chapter Preview Links

### Overview
Writers need a lightweight, private way to get feedback on draft chapters from trusted readers without granting edit access. This spec defines the sharing flow, preview page experience for readers, and feedback surface for writers.

---

### 1. Share Preview Action

**Entry point:** Per-chapter action menu (three-dot or right-click) in the story outline/chapter list: Share preview

**Share controls dialog:**
- Chapter title (read-only display)
- Preview link: auto-generated URL displayed with Copy button
- Expiration date: optional date picker (default: no expiration)
- Regenerate link button: creates a new link URL (old link immediately invalidated)
- Revoke link button: permanently deactivates the link (with confirmation)
- Reaction checkpoints toggle: Enable structured checkpoints (links to BIN-108 feature when implemented)

**State persistence:**
- Active links shown with: status badge (Active / Expired / Revoked), creation date, expiration date (if set)
- Writers can manage multiple active preview links per chapter (max 3 per chapter in v1)

---

### 2. Preview Page (Reader View)

**URL pattern:** /preview/[unique-token]

**Page layout:**
- App name/branding header (minimal; no navigation to writer workspace)
- Chapter title
- Chapter content (read-only; no selection text copy in mobile view)
- End-of-chapter feedback section (if checkpoints enabled; see BIN-108)
- Short comment field: Leave a comment (optional freeform, max 500 chars)
- Submit reaction: thumbs up / thumbs down (required before submitting comment)
- Submit button

**No account required** for readers — anonymous session

**Expired/revoked links:**
- Show a clean error page: This preview is no longer available. with no content exposure

---

### 3. Feedback in Writer View

**Feedback entries surface in chapter sidebar:**
- New tab or section: Preview Feedback
- Each entry shows: reader session ID (anonymous, e.g., Reader 1, Reader 2), reaction (thumbs up/down), comment text, date
- Badge on chapter in outline when new feedback arrives (unread count)

**No moderation tooling in v1** (out of scope per non-goals)

---

### 4. Security and Privacy
- Preview links use cryptographically random tokens (not guessable)
- Expired tokens: checked server-side on each request; no cached content served
- Chapter content is not indexed or discoverable; no public search surface
- Reader submissions are tied to the token, not to any reader account

---

### 5. Empty States

- No active preview links: Share a chapter for private feedback. Create your first preview link.
- Link created, no feedback yet: Waiting for reader feedback. Share this link with trusted readers.
- Link revoked/expired: This preview link is no longer active. Create a new one if needed.

---

### 6. Accessibility and Performance
- Copy link button has keyboard focus and success confirmation (toast: Link copied)
- Preview page is lightweight (no editor bundle loaded; content-only render)
- Feedback submission is a single form POST; no JS framework dependency required

---

### 7. Open Questions for Engineering
1. Token length and entropy: 32-char URL-safe random string sufficient?
2. Max 3 active links per chapter: enforced server-side?
3. Anonymous reader identity: session cookie per token visit, or per-token per-browser fingerprint?
4. Chapter content rendering: re-use existing read-only renderer or new lightweight component?

---

### Handoff
Design complete. Ready for engineering.

- Source issue: [BIN-70](/BIN/issues/BIN-70)

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This is worth building if positioned as a private, chapter-scoped critique lane: faster than full collaboration suites and safer/cleaner than public community feedback. The differentiator is trust + focus (invite-only read links, revocation/expiry, lightweight reactions in-context), not just "share link" parity.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter already supports chapter sharing via link (baseline parity): https://livingwriter.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4414842027666-Share-Chapter-via-Link
- Campfire markets private guest share links and collaborator invites for drafts (baseline parity): https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt supports inline feedback and emoji reactions directly in story text (engagement benchmark): https://inkitt.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21022248999698-Inline-Feedback-on-Inkitt

## Risks / trade-offs
- Can become a generic clone if we add full social/community mechanics too early.
- Anonymous/open links increase spam/moderation burden unless tightly scoped.
- Feedback volume could become noisy without simple signal prioritization in the writer view.

## Recommendation
Approve, with product/design guardrails: keep feedback private by default, constrain to chapter-level invite links, and optimize for actionable revision signals over social chatter.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Update

Requesting Market Research approval/filter pass before engineering intake.

- Source goal: [Write a story writing app](/BIN/issues/BIN-70)
- Review ask: approve/reject/reframe with competitor-backed differentiation (Living Writer/Campfire/Inkitt)
- Next: if approved, we will route to planning/design in priority order
